headlines.title: Shell Moves to Exit French Fuel Retail Market in Strategic Pivot
headlines.summary: Shell plans to sell its network of about 60 service stations in France in a deal that could be finalized early next year, French daily Les Echos reported on Tuesday, quoting a communication the UK-based supermajor has shared with the affected employees, suppliers, and concessiona

headlines.title: China’s LNG Imports Rebound From Eight-Year Low
headlines.summary: China’s imports of liquefied natural gas are on the mend, with the 30-day moving average rising to the highest level since late February, Bloomberg reported today, citing shipping data. headlines.title: Oil Prices Rise as U.S.-Iran Peace Hopes Fade
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices ticked higher today as hopes for a peace deal between the United States and Iran faded further, after President Trump called Iran’s response to a U.S. peace deal proposal “garbage”. headlines.title: After Squandering $25B In Refinery Overhauls, Nigeria Turns To Chinese Firms
headlines.summary: After a series of failed and costly attempts to revamp its aging refineries, Nigeria’s national oil company, the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPC), has signed a new agreement with Chinese firms to revive its moribund facilities. headlines.title: Aramco Sees Slow Oil Market Recovery after Shock Supply Loss
headlines.summary: The oil market will take months to normalize even if flows through the Strait of Hormuz resumed today, as 1 billion barrels of oil have been wiped off the supply balance over the past two and a half months. headlines.title: IEA: Portugal Energy Poverty Above EU Average, Despite Renewable Boom

headlines.title: Reuters Survey Shows OPEC Oil Output At 26-Year Low Amid Iran War
